req.title: Manager creates performance-review
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a performance-review through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-021 requires an authenticated manager to create performance-reviews via REST API with mandatory field validation and audit logging. The test suite applies Equivalence Partitioning to partition valid/invalid input states, Boundary Value Analysis to test field length limits, Negative Path testing for authentication/authorization failures, Decision Table logic for mandatory field combinations, and State Transition Coverage to verify audit record creation. Tests are designed to be deterministic, idempotent, and independently executable.

tests.name: test_manager_creates_performance_review_success
preconditions: Manager is authenticated with valid JWT token
actions: Payload contains mandatory fields: employee_id, review_period, rating
Verify response status and response body
expected: Audit record is written with action='CREATE' and resource_type='performance_review'
trace: R-WEB-021: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
def test_manager_creates_performance_review_success(auth_headers_manager, api_base_url):
    payload = {
        "employee_id": "emp-001",
        "review_period": "2024-Q1",
        "rating": 4,
        "comments": "Exceeds expectations"
    }
    resp = <request_helper>.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/performance-reviews",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers_manager
    )
    assert resp.status_code in [200, 201]
    data = resp.json()
    assert "id" in data or "performance_review_id" in data
    assert data.get("created_by") is not None
    audit = <audit_helper>.get_latest_record("performance_review")
    assert audit["action"] == "CREATE"
    assert audit["resource_type"] == "performance_review"

tests.name: test_manager_missing_mandatory_field_employee_id
preconditions: Manager is authenticated with valid JWT token
actions: Payload contains review_period and rating but not employee_id
Verify response status and error message
expected: Error message indicates employee_id is mandatory
trace: R-WEB-021: error pathway 400 on validation error
tests.executable:
def test_manager_missing_mandatory_field_employee_id(auth_headers_manager, api_base_url):
    payload = {
        "review_period": "2024-Q1",
        "rating": 4
    }
    resp = <request_helper>.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/performance-reviews",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers_manager
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    data = resp.json()
    assert "employee_id" in data.get("errors", {})
    assert <db_helper>.count_performance_reviews() == 0
    audit = <audit_helper>.get_records_by_action("CREATE")
    assert len(audit) == 0

def test_boundary_rating_field_min_value(auth_headers_manager, api_base_url):
    payload = {
        "employee_id": "emp-001",
        "review_period": "2024-Q1",
        "rating": 1
    }
    resp = <request_helper>.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/performance-reviews",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers_manager
    )
    assert resp.status_code in [200, 201]
    data = resp.json()
    assert data.get("rating") == 1

tests.name: test_boundary_comments_field_exceeds_max_length
preconditions: Manager is authenticated with valid JWT token
Comments field has max length of 500 characters
expected: Error message indicates comments exceeds max length
trace: R-WEB-021: boundary value analysis exceeding max length
R-WEB-021: error pathway 400 on validation error
tests.executable:
def test_boundary_comments_field_exceeds_max_length(auth_headers_manager, api_base_url):
    payload = {
        "employee_id": "emp-001",
        "review_period": "2024-Q1",
        "rating": 4,
        "comments": "x" * 501
    }
    resp = <request_helper>.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/performance-reviews",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers_manager
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    assert "comments" in resp.json().get("errors", {})
